items.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early-to-mid 2026. Key model changes include the launch of Claude Opus 4.7, their most capable generally available model for complex reasoning and agentic coding, at the same $5/$25 per MTok pricing as Opus 4.6. Claude Haiku 3 (claude-3-haiku-20240307) is being deprecated with retirement on April 19, 2026, and the 1M token context window beta for Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Claude Sonnet 4 is retiring April 30, 2026 — developers should migrate

items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7, a less risky model than Mythos
items.author: www.cnbc.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been on an aggressive release cadence in early 2026. On April 16, 2026, the company released Claude Opus 4.7, a major upgrade featuring stronger software engineering performance, improved vision capabilities (processing images at up to 2,576 pixels on the long edge — more than 3x the resolution of prior models), sharper instruction following, and more reliable long-running agent work. The model also introduces a new 'xhigh' effort level, giving users finer control over the reasonin

items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7, concedes it trails unreleased Mythos — Axios
items.author: www.axios.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's most recent announcements (as of April 18, 2026) center on two major releases. On April 16, Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7, a general-availability model with improvements in software engineering, long-running coding tasks, and higher-resolution vision. Alongside it, Anthropic quietly unveiled Claude Mythos Preview — its most powerful model, focused on cybersecurity (identifying software weaknesses and security flaws) — but kept its release restricted to select companies rather th
